REPLY SUPPORTING DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT AXIS SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY, ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS AXIS INSURANCE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT AXIS SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT IT HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A PRESENT DUTY TO DEFEND AND REQUESTS LEAVE TO AMEND. (OPP., P. 2:6-17.) FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE EQUITABLE CONTRIBUTION. "TH[E] RIGHT OF EQUITABLE CONTRIBUTION BELONGS TO EACH INSURER INDIVIDUALLY. IT IS NOT BASED ON ANY RIGHT OF SUBROGATION TO THE RIGHTS OF THE INSURED, AND IS NOT EQUIVALENT TO " 'STANDING IN THE SHOES' " OF THE INSURED." (FIREMAN'S FUND INS. CO. V. MARYLAND CAS. CO. (1998) 65 CAL.APP.4TH 1279, 1294.) THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT UNCERTAIN. (CCP 430.10(F).) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-30-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
R. Scott La Salle, Esq. of The Aguilera Law Group, APLC, Tel. 714.384.6600, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT AXIS SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the first cause of action. Overruled as to the second cause of action. For the first cause of action for declaratory relief, Plaintiff concedes that it has not pled sufficient facts to allege a present duty to defend and requests leave to amend. (Opp., p. 2:6-17.) For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege equitable contribution. "Th[e] right of equitable contribution belongs to each insurer individually. It is not based on any right of subrogation to the rights of the insured, and is not equivalent to " 'standing in the shoes' " of the insured." (Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Maryland Cas. Co. (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 1279, 1294.) The second cause of action is not uncertain. (CCP 430.10(f).) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
